HELIOS - HomogEneous and Layered medIa Optical Scattering
C++ surface integral equation (SIE) code for homogeneous and layered media scattering

SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp File Reference

Implementation of SommerfeldIntegrator: contour construction, integrand assembly, QUADPACK integration, and interpolation packing. More...

#include "SommerfeldIntegrator.h"
#include <algorithm>
#include <numeric>
#include <unordered_map>

Detailed Description

Implementation of SommerfeldIntegrator: contour construction, integrand assembly, QUADPACK integration, and interpolation packing.

Definition in file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

dcmplx besselJ ( int order,

◆ dqage_()

void dqage_ ( double(* f )(double *),
double * a,
double * b,
double * epsabs,
double * epsrel,
int * key,
int * limit,
double * result,
double * abserr,
int * neval,
int * ier,
double * alist,
double * blist,
double * rlist,
double * elist,
int * iord,
int * last )

Adaptive Gauss–Kronrod quadrature (QUADPACK driver routine DQAGE).

Evaluates a real integral using adaptive Gauss–Kronrod rules over a finite interval.

Parameters
fPointer to the function to integrate.
aLower integration bound.
bUpper integration bound.
epsabsAbsolute accuracy requested.
epsrelRelative accuracy requested.
keyRule selection key (1–6 corresponding to different Gauss–Kronrod pairs).
limitMaximum number of subintervals allowed.
resultOutput integral result.
abserrEstimated absolute error.
nevalNumber of function evaluations performed.
ierError flag (0 if successful).
alistWorkspace array of left subinterval bounds.
blistWorkspace array of right subinterval bounds.
rlistWorkspace array of subinterval results.
elistWorkspace array of subinterval errors.
iordWorkspace array for ordering subintervals.
lastNumber of subintervals actually used.
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ gaussKronrod()

dcmplx gaussKronrod ( double(* integrand )(double *),
double a,
double b,
int key = 5,
int limit = 100,
double epsabs = 1e-6,
double epsrel = 1e-6 )

Performs Gauss-Kronrod integration.

Parameters
integrandFunction pointer to the integrand function.
aLower bound of integration.
bUpper bound of integration.
keySelection of Gauss-Kronrod pair (5 -> 25/51).
limitMaximum subdivisions.
epsabsAbsolute error tolerance.
epsrelRelative error tolerance.
Returns
Computed integral value as a complex number.

◆ getColumn()

std::vector< double > getColumn ( const std::vector< std::vector< double > > & matrix,
size_t colIndex )

Extract a single column from a 2D matrix.

Parameters
matrixInput 2D matrix (matrix[row][col]).
colIndexIndex of the column to extract.
Returns
A vector containing all elements from the specified column.
Exceptions
std::out_of_rangeif colIndex is greater than the number of columns.

◆ getColumnAtRowDepth()

std::vector< double > getColumnAtRowDepth ( const std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & matrix,
size_t rowIndex,
size_t depthIndex )

Extract one row (across all columns) at a specific depth in a 3D matrix.

Parameters
matrixInput 3D matrix (matrix[row][col][depth]).
rowIndexRow index to extract.
depthIndexDepth (third-dimension) index to extract.
Returns
A vector of values at the specified (row, :, depth).
Exceptions
std::out_of_rangeif indices are out of bounds.

rowIndexRow index to extract.

int kind,

◆ interpolateInter()

std::tuple< std::vector< double >, std::vector< double >, std::vector< double >, std::vector< dcmplx > > interpolateInter ( std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & x,
std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & y,
std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< double > > > & z,
std::vector< double > & inScales,
std::vector< std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > > & green )

Flatten 3D complex tensor grid (column-major order).

Parameters
xA 3D vector representing the x-coordinates of the grid.
yA 3D vector representing the y-coordinates of the grid.
zA 3D vector representing the z-coordinates of the grid.
inScalesA vector with the scaling factors for x, y, z coordinates.
greenA 3D vector containing complex function values at grid points.
Returns
A tuple containing:
  • Xv: A vector of x-coordinates.
  • Yv: A vector of y-coordinates.
  • Zv: A vector of z-coordinates.
  • F_flat: green flattened in column-major order.

Definition at line 479 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ interpolateIntra()

std::tuple< std::vector< dcmplx >, std::vector< int >, std::vector< int >, std::vector< double >, std::vector< double > > interpolateIntra ( std::vector< double > & x_grid,
std::vector< double > & y_grid,
std::vector< std::vector< dcmplx > > & green )

Flatten 2D complex grid to column-major array with axis metadata.

Parameters
x_gridA vector containing the x-coordinates of the grid.
y_gridA vector containing the y-coordinates of the grid.
greenA 2D vector containing the complex values of the function on the grid.
Returns
A tuple containing:
  • F_flat: green flattened in column-major order.
  • px: A vector of two integers representing px values for real and imaginary parts.
  • py: A vector of two integers representing py values for real and imaginary parts.
  • Xc: x_grid points.
  • Yc: y_grid points.

Definition at line 441 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ pickImagMember()

double pickImagMember ( double * x)

Retrieves the imaginary part of a selected member of the computed integrand.

This function selects the correct member from either the "inter" or "intra" integrand, computing it if necessary and caching the result for future lookups.

Parameters
xPointer to the independent variable for which the integrand is evaluated.
Returns
The imaginary part of the selected member of the computed integrand.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif g_status is neither "inter" nor "intra".

Definition at line 167 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ pickRealMember()

double pickRealMember ( double * x)

Retrieves the real part of a selected member of the computed integrand.

This function selects the correct member from either the "inter" or "intra" integrand, computing it if necessary and caching the result for future lookups.

Parameters
xPointer to the independent variable for which the integrand is evaluated.
Returns
The real part of the selected member of the computed integrand.
Exceptions
std::invalid_argumentif g_status is neither "inter" nor "intra".

Definition at line 78 of file SommerfeldIntegrator.cpp.

Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ zbesh_()

void zbesh_ ( const double * zr,
const double * zi,
const double * fnu,
const int * kode,
const int * m,
const int * n,
double * cyr,
double * cyi,
int * nz,
int * ierr )

Compute complex Hankel functions of the first kind or second kind \( H_\nu^{(m)}(z) \).

Fortran wrapper to the Amos library routine ZBESH.

Parameters
zrReal part of the complex argument z.
ziImaginary part of the complex argument z.
fnuOrder of the Hankel function.
kodeScaling option (1 = unscaled, 2 = exponentially scaled).
mType selector (1 = first kind, 2 = second kind).
nNumber of sequential orders to compute.
cyrOutput array for the real parts of \( H_\nu^{(m)}(z) \).
cyiOutput array for the imaginary parts of \( H_\nu^{(m)}(z) \).
nzNumber of underflows set to zero.
ierrError flag (0 if successful).
Here is the caller graph for this function:

◆ zbesj_()

void zbesj_ ( const double * zr,
const double * zi,
const double * fnu,
const int * kode,
const int * n,
double * cyr,
double * cyi,
int * nz,
int * ierr )

Compute complex Bessel functions of the first kind \( J_\nu(z) \).

Fortran wrapper to the Amos library routine ZBESJ.

Parameters
zrReal part of the complex argument z.
ziImaginary part of the complex argument z.
fnuOrder of the Bessel function.
kodeScaling option (1 = unscaled, 2 = exponentially scaled).
nNumber of terms to compute (number of sequential orders).
cyrOutput array for the real parts of \( J_\nu(z) \).
cyiOutput array for the imaginary parts of \( J_\nu(z) \).
nzNumber of underflows set to zero.
ierrError flag (0 if successful).
Here is the caller graph for this function:
